summary: Mode 2b singleton verification of SEC-6.13 (MOND Grouping) finds 5 WIN reclassifications (047/048/052/054/055 → Self-Contradicted) are accurate and prose intent is preserved — but the section contains an internal arithmetic inconsistency that pre-dates the recent EXP-295/EXP-427 integration. TLDR says "Five of the eleven WINs added in late March 2026" + "The remaining six (5 tidal + 044/053)". The explicit lists contain 5 MOND-list + 5 tidal + 2 dome-internal = 12 WINs, not 11; and the listed "remaining" group is 7 items, not 6. Body second mention repeats "The remaining six belong to adjacent but distinct categories" with the same 7-item enumeration. Body also says "between WIN-044 and WIN-054" (which is 11 WINs inclusive) but WIN-055 is repeatedly placed in the MOND-list. Either WIN-055 was in the same commit burst (in which case range should be 044-055 and total 12) or it was added separately (in which case TLDR "Five of the eleven" misclassifies WIN-055 into the burst). Verification of the actual dome-side commits was not feasible from our repo; the safest fix decouples the burst-count from the MOND-grouping count.
detail: Verification background: the attention-inbox item asked the analyst to verify (a) prose intent preserved and (b) five WIN reclassifications (047/048/052/054/055 → Self-Contradicted) accurately summarized. Verdict alignment confirmed in data/wins.json for all five MOND-list WINs and the seven other WINs in the discussed cohort (WIN-044/045/046/049/050/051/053 — all also Self-Contradicted). The kernel-of-truth h3 from EXP-427 is integrated and reads as intended. The argumentative arc (kernel → list-match → closed-world → placement → deeper lesson) is intact. The arithmetic inconsistency is a small prose-cleanup item that does not affect the substantive argument or any per-WIN reclassification, but produces a "5 + 6 = 11 but the explicit lists add to 12" reader-noticeable error in two places (TLDR and body). Mode 2b singleton resolves the attention-inbox request; this proposal flags the arithmetic for a separate quick patch.

patches.old: Five of the eleven WINs added in late March 2026 (WIN-047, 048, 052, 054, 055) trace item-for-item to the modified-gravity / MOND community's canonical anomaly list — the Radial Acceleration Relation, El Gordo, the CMB Axis of Evil, Hubble tension, Cepheid tension. The remaining six (tidal constituents WIN-045/046/049/050/051 and dome-internal derivations WIN-044/053) fall under adjacent but distinct patterns: standard physics relabeled as dome predictions, and dome-internal failed derivations.
patches.new: Five WINs from the late-March 2026 cohort (WIN-047, 048, 052, 054, 055) trace item-for-item to the modified-gravity / MOND community's canonical anomaly list — the Radial Acceleration Relation, El Gordo, the CMB Axis of Evil, Hubble tension, Cepheid tension. Seven other WINs in the same cohort (tidal constituents WIN-045/046/049/050/051 and dome-internal derivations WIN-044/053) fall under adjacent but distinct patterns: standard physics relabeled as dome predictions, and dome-internal failed derivations.
patches.rationale: Decouples burst-count (uncertain — body specifies WIN-044 through WIN-054 inclusive in three 2026-03-23 morning commits, which is 11 WINs, but the MOND-list explicitly includes WIN-055 which would push the cohort to 12) from the MOND-grouping count (5). Removes the "of the eleven" framing that produced 5+6=11 arithmetic inconsistent with the 5+7=12 enumeration. Replaces "remaining six" with "Seven other WINs in the same cohort" to match the actual listed count (5 tidal + 044/053 = 7).

patches.target_entry: part6.html — body of p6-613-mond-grouping, h3 "The list, and why list-match matters"
patches.old: and Cepheid distance-ladder tension (WIN-055). The remaining six belong to adjacent but distinct categories: tidal constituents M2/S2/K1/O1/N2 (WIN-045/046/049/050/051) are well-explained standard geophysical phenomena not addressed by MOND; a custom-derived firmament scaling function (WIN-044) and two-pole geomagnetic model (WIN-053) are dome-internal derivations whose refutations rest on their own terms.
patches.new: and Cepheid distance-ladder tension (WIN-055). The remaining seven belong to adjacent but distinct categories: tidal constituents M2/S2/K1/O1/N2 (WIN-045/046/049/050/051) are well-explained standard geophysical phenomena not addressed by MOND; a custom-derived firmament scaling function (WIN-044) and two-pole geomagnetic model (WIN-053) are dome-internal derivations whose refutations rest on their own terms.
patches.rationale: Fix "remaining six" → "remaining seven" to match the actual enumeration (5 tidal + 044/053 = 7). The body lists seven items immediately after the "six" count; the count is wrong, the enumeration is right. Single-word fix; no other prose change needed in this paragraph.

patches.target_entry: part6.html — body of p6-613-mond-grouping, h3 "The list, and why list-match matters" (first paragraph)
patches.old: The eleven WINs added between WIN-044 and WIN-054 in three commits on the morning of 2026-03-23 (04:57, 05:14, and 05:29 UTC — 32 minutes from first to last) are not a heterogeneous selection of dome-cosmology insights that happen to coincide with MOND-community talking points.
patches.new: The WINs added between WIN-044 and WIN-055 in the late-March 2026 commit cluster (the body of which lands in three commits on the morning of 2026-03-23 at 04:57, 05:14, and 05:29 UTC — 32 minutes from first to last) are not a heterogeneous selection of dome-cosmology insights that happen to coincide with MOND-community talking points.
patches.rationale: Reconciles the body range with the MOND-list inclusion of WIN-055. If dome-side commit log confirms WIN-055 was added in one of the three 2026-03-23 commits, prefer the alternative: change "between WIN-044 and WIN-054" to "between WIN-044 and WIN-055" and "eleven WINs" → "twelve WINs". The current phrasing in patch P3.new is safer — it does not commit to whether WIN-055 was in one of the named three commits, only that it is in the late-March 2026 cohort. Decider should pick: (i) apply P3 as written (no specific count, cohort framing), OR (ii) verify dome-side git log and apply count-explicit alternative.
patches.alt_new_if_dome_log_confirms_12_in_burst: The twelve WINs added between WIN-044 and WIN-055 in three commits on the morning of 2026-03-23 (04:57, 05:14, and 05:29 UTC — 32 minutes from first to last) are not a heterogeneous selection of dome-cosmology insights that happen to coincide with MOND-community talking points.

anticipated_objections.objection: The arithmetic inconsistency does not change any per-WIN reclassification or the substantive argument of SEC-6.13. Why patch?
anticipated_objections.response: Reader-noticeable arithmetic error in load-bearing prose (TLDR + body first paragraph). The site's critique of the dome model rests partly on the dome's own internal inconsistencies; carrying a 5+6=11-but-actually-12 inconsistency in our own prose erodes credibility on exactly the dimension we critique. Minor severity, one or two-word fixes.

anticipated_objections.objection: EXP-427 was just integrated. Touching SEC-6.13 again risks churning recently-stable content.
anticipated_objections.response: P1/P2 are pure word-substitutions ({eleven, remaining six} → {decouple-and-seven, seven}) that do not touch argument structure or any of EXP-427's kernel-of-truth content. P3 is the only structural touch (decouples burst-range from MOND-list cohort) and is necessary to resolve the WIN-055-in-burst-or-not question. If the decider considers churn risk decisive, P1 and P2 can land without P3 and the section will be self-consistent (5 + 7 = 12, no explicit "eleven" count to contradict the range).

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not just have the analyst verify dome-side git for the WIN-055 commit?
anticipated_objections.response: Dome-side git log lives in the dome's repo at github.com/Nhughes09/predictions — not directly accessible without an external fetch outside the standard analyst toolchain. Curl-fetching the dome git log on every prose-arithmetic patch is not in the standard procedure, and the P3 framing ("late-March 2026 commit cluster") is dome-source-agnostic.

anticipated_objections.objection: Counter-argument from the operator's preference: "Think about the other side." The other side here is that this is minor prose polish on a section that already has correct substance — and we could simply leave it.
anticipated_objections.response: Acknowledged. If the decider deems P1/P2/P3 not worth the integration cost, dismissing this proposal is a reasonable outcome and would not invalidate any review claim. The verification finding (per-WIN verdicts and prose intent preserved) stands regardless; the patch is opt-in.
